Vietnam Trip

2026-03-13 → 2026-03-26
Adventure + Architecture + Living History

2026-03-13 – Ho Chi Minh City

Arrival + Colonial Axis + First Night Energy

First immersion into Saigon's urban rhythm. Today is about walking, absorbing scale, and transitioning into Vietnam gently without overloading.

Afternoon: Arrival and check-in
Base yourself in District 1 for maximum walkability and architectural density.
Location: District 1, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=District+1+Ho+Chi+Minh+City+Vietnam

Nguyễn Huệ Walking Street
The city's central promenade — wide, modern, energetic. A perfect first contact with Saigon's scale and pace.
Location: Nguyen Hue Walking Street, Ho Chi Minh City
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Nguyen+Hue+Walking+Street+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

Café Apartment (42 Nguyen Hue)
A vertical maze of indie cafés inside an old apartment block — layered urbanism at its best.
Location: 42 Nguyen Hue Apartment Building
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=42+Nguyen+Hue+Apartment+Building+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

Đồng Khởi Street
Colonial boulevard with French façades — this is where Saigon's historic wealth concentrated.
Location: Dong Khoi Street
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Dong+Khoi+Street+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

Optional Evening: Yoko Café
Intimate live music venue with a local crowd — good soft landing into nightlife.
Location: Yoko Cafe, District 3
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Yoko+Cafe+District+3+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

2026-03-14 – Ho Chi Minh City

State Power + War Memory + Night Pulse

Today contrasts political architecture and historical trauma with vibrant nightlife.

Reunification Palace
Frozen-in-time 1970s modernism — symbol of the fall of Saigon and reunification.
Location: Independence Palace
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Independence+Palace+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

Notre Dame Cathedral & Central Post Office
French colonial presence in architectural form — red brick cathedral and Eiffel-influenced post office.
Location: Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Notre+Dame+Cathedral+Basilica+of+Saigon

War Remnants Museum
Heavy but essential — Vietnam War from the Vietnamese perspective.
Location: War Remnants Museum
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=War+Remnants+Museum+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

Evening: Thi Bar
One of Saigon's established queer-friendly nightlife anchors — live performances, strong Saturday energy.
Location: Thi Bar Saigon
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Thi+Bar+Saigon

2026-03-15 – Ho Chi Minh City

Chợ Lớn + Diaspora + Street Energy

Today shifts to Chinese-Vietnamese heritage and urban density.

Binh Tay Market
Wholesale chaos, architecture, and commerce layered together.
Location: Binh Tay Market
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Binh+Tay+Market+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

Thien Hau Pagoda
Incense coils, red lanterns, maritime goddess worship — atmospheric and photogenic.
Location: Thien Hau Temple
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Thien+Hau+Temple+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

Optional: Pride Café
Community-led queer space — softer alternative to club nightlife.
Location: Pride Cafe HCMC
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pride+Cafe+Ho+Chi+Minh+City

2026-03-16 – Cu Chi + Art + Flight North

History intensifies before heading central.

Củ Chi Tunnels (Ben Dược site preferred)
Underground resistance network — claustrophobic, powerful insight into wartime strategy.
Location: Ben Duoc Cu Chi Tunnels
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Ben+Duoc+Cu+Chi+Tunnels

Fine Arts Museum
Colonial mansion hosting Vietnamese modern and contemporary art — emotional decompression after Cu Chi.
Location: Ho Chi Minh City Museum of Fine Arts
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Ho+Chi+Minh+City+Museum+of+Fine+Arts

2026-03-17 – Huế

Imperial Geometry + River Atmosphere

Vietnam's former imperial capital — slower, poetic, architectural.

Imperial City (UNESCO)
Citadel walls, palaces, dynastic planning — Vietnam's political heart for centuries.
Location: Imperial City Hue
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Imperial+City+Hue

Evening: Nguyễn Đình Chiểu Walking Street
River promenade with softer, local energy.
Location: Nguyen Dinh Chieu Walking Street Hue
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Nguyen+Dinh+Chieu+Walking+Street+Hue

2026-03-18 – Tomb Architecture + Scenic Train

Khải Định Tomb
Dramatic blend of Vietnamese and European styles — maximalist and theatrical.
Location: Khai Dinh Tomb
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Khai+Dinh+Tomb+Hue

Tự Đức Tomb
Serene, poetic, integrated into landscape.
Location: Tu Duc Tomb
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Tu+Duc+Tomb+Hue

Scenic Train Hue → Da Nang
Coastal mountain route — one of Southeast Asia's most beautiful rail journeys.

2026-03-21 – Hanoi

Slow Day + Wild Night

Architectural wandering by day, electronic escalation by night.

Old Quarter Loop
Medieval street grid, guild streets, French overlays.
Location: Old Quarter Hanoi
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Old+Quarter+Hanoi

GC Bar
Hanoi's most established gay bar — social anchor before the night intensifies.
Location: GC Bar Hanoi
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=GC+Bar+Hanoi

Unmute with Surusinghe
International electronic event — the designated "wild night."
Location: 169 Phuc Tan, Hoan Kiem, Hanoi
Google Maps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=169+Phuc+Tan+Hanoi
